title: Swift Transformers 1.0 版本发布——展望未来
author: Pedro Cuenca, Christopher Fleetwood, Mattt, Vaibhav Srivastav
content:
We released swift-transformers two years ago (!) with the goal to support Apple developers and help them integrate local LLMs in their apps. A lot has changed since then (MLX and chat templates did not exist!), and we’ve learned how the community is actually using the library.

We want to double down on the use cases that provide most benefits to the community, and lay out the foundations for the future. Spoiler alert: after this release, we’ll focus a lot on MLX and agentic use cases 🚀

What is swift-transformers

swift-transformers is a Swift library that aims to reduce the friction for developers that want to work with local models on Apple Silicon platforms, including iPhones. It includes the missing pieces that are not provided by Core ML or MLX alone, but that are required to work with local inference. Namely, it provides the following components:

  • Tokenizers. Preparing inputs for a language model is surprisingly complex. We've built a lot of experience with our tokenizers Python and Rust libraries, which are foundational to the AI ecosystem. We wanted to bring the same performant, ergonomic experience to Swift. The Swift version of Tokenizers should handle everything for you, including chat templates and agentic use!
  • Hub. This is an interface to the Hugging Face Hub, where all open models are available. It allows you to download models from the Hub and cache them locally, and supports background resumable downloads, model updates, offline mode. It contains a subset of the functionality provided by the Python and JavaScript libraries, focused on the tasks that Apple developers need the most (i.e., uploads are not supported).
  • Models and Generation. These are wrappers for LLMs converted to the Core ML format. Converting them is out of the scope of the library (but we have some guides). Once they are converted, these modules make it easy to run inference with them.

How is the community using it

Most of the time people use the Tokenizers or Hub modules, and frequently both. Some notable projects that rely on swift-transformers include:

  • mlx-swift-examples, by Apple. It’s, in fact, not just a collection of examples, but a list of libraries you can use to run various types of models using MLX, including LLMs and VLMs (vision-language models). It’s kind of our Models and Generation libraries but for MLX instead of Core ML – and it supports many more model types like embedders or Stable Diffusion.
  • WhisperKit, by argmax. Open Source ASR (speech recognition) framework, super heavily optimized for Apple Silicon. It relies on our Hub and Tokenizers modules.
  • FastVLM, by Apple, and many other app demos, such as our own SmolVLM2 native app.

What changes with v1.0

Version 1.0 signals stability in the package. Developers are building apps on swift-transformers, and this first major release recognizes those use cases and brings the version number in line with that reality. It also provides the foundation on which to iterate with the community to build the next set of features. These are some of our preferred updates:

  • Tokenizers and Hub are now first-citizen, top-level modules. Before 1.0, you had to depend on and import the full package, whereas now you can just pick Tokenizers, for instance.
  • Speaking of Jinja, we are super proud to announce that we have collaborated with John Mai (X) to create the next version of his excellent Swift Jinja library. John’s work has been crucial for the community: he single-handedly took on the task to provide a solid chat template library that could grow as templates became more and more complex. The new version is a couple orders of magnitude faster (no kidding), and lives here as swift-jinja.
  • To further reduce the load imposed on downstream users, we have removed our example CLI targets and the swift-argument-parser dependency, which in turn prevents version conflicts for projects that already use it.
  • Thanks to contributions by Apple, we have adopted Modern Core ML APIs with support for stateful models (for easier KV-caching) and expressive MLTensor APIs – this removes thousands of lines of custom tensor operations and math code.
  • Lots of additional cruft removed and API surface reduced to reduce cognitive load and iterate faster.
  • Tests are better, faster, stronger.
  • Documentation comments have been added to public APIs.
  • Swift 6 is fully supported.

Version 1.0 comes with breaking API changes. However, we don’t expect major problems if you are a user of Tokenizers or Hub. If you use the Core ML components of the library, please get in touch so we can support you during transition. We’ll prepare a migration guide and add it to the documentation.

Usage Examples

Here's how to use Tokenizers to format tool calling input for an LLM:

import Tokenizers

let tokenizer = try await AutoTokenizer.from(pretrained: "mlx-community/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct-4bit")

let weatherTool = [
    "type": "function",
    "function": [
        "name": "get_current_weather",
        "description": "Get the current weather in a given location",
        "parameters": [
            "type": "object",
            "properties": ["location": ["type": "string", "description": "City and state"]],
            "required": ["location"]
        ]
    ]
]

let tokens = try tokenizer.applyChatTemplate(
    messages: [["role": "user", "content": "What's the weather in Paris?"]],
    tools: [weatherTool]
)

For additional examples, please check this section in the README and the Examples folder.

What comes next

Honestly, we don’t know. We do know that we are super interested in exploring MLX, because that’s usually the current go-to approach for developers getting started with ML in native apps, and we want to help make the experience as seamless as possible. We are thinking along the lines of better integration with mlx-swift-examples for LLMs and VLMs, potentially through pre-processing and post-processing operations that developers encounter frequently.

We are also extremely excited about agentic use in general and MCP in particular. We think that exposure of system resources to local workflows would be 🚀

If you want to follow along in this journey or want to share your ideas, please contact us through our social networks or the repo.

We couldn’t have done this without you 🫵

We are immensely grateful to all the contributors and users of the library for your help and feedback. We love you all, and can't wait to continue working with you to shape the future of on-device generation! ❤️
aiSummary: Hugging Face 的 `swift-transformers` 达到 v1.0 版本，提供稳定的模块化组件，用于在 Apple 平台集成本地 LLM，未来重点关注 MLX 和自主智能 (Agentic AI)。
oneSentenceSummary: Hugging Face 的 `swift-transformers` 达到 v1.0 版本，提供稳定的模块化组件，用于在 Apple 平台集成本地 LLM，未来重点关注 MLX 和自主智能 (Agentic AI)。
summary: 本文宣布了 `swift-transformers` 的 1.0 版本发布。`swift-transformers` 是一个由 Hugging Face 开发的 Swift 库，旨在简化将本地大型语言模型 (LLM) 集成到应用程序中的过程。这些应用程序运行在包括 iPhones 在内的 Apple 芯片平台上。它重点介绍了该库的核心组件：用于复杂输入准备的 `Tokenizers`、用于从 Hugging Face 下载和缓存模型的 `Hub`，以及用于 Core ML 转换的 LLM 的 `Models` 和 `Generation` 封装器。文章指出，社区主要使用 `Tokenizers` 和 `Hub`，并引用了 Apple 的 `mlx-swift-examples` 和 `argmax` 的 WhisperKit 等项目。1.0 版本标志着稳定性，引入了关键更新，例如模块化的 `Tokenizers` 和 `Hub` 作为顶级模块、速度明显更快的 `swift-jinja` 库、采用用于有状态模型的 Modern Core ML API (现代 Core ML API) 以及精简的 API 设计。展望未来，重点将转向与 MLX 的更深入集成，并探索自主智能 (Agentic AI) 用例，旨在实现无缝的开发者体验。

mainPoints.point: `swift-transformers` 通过提供必要的组件，简化了 Apple 开发者集成本地 LLM 的流程。
mainPoints.explanation: 该库提供用于输入准备的 `Tokenizers`、用于模型管理的 `Hub` 以及用于 Core ML 推理的 `Models` / `Generation`，填补了现有 Apple 机器学习框架中的空白，并简化了设备端 AI 的开发。

mainPoints.point: 1.0 版本为该库带来了更高的稳定性、更强的模块化设计和一系列重大技术改进。
mainPoints.explanation: 主要更新包括将 `Tokenizers` 和 `Hub` 作为顶级模块、用于聊天模板的更快的 `swift-jinja`、采用 Modern Core ML API (现代 Core ML API) 以及整体 API 改进，从而增强了开发者体验和性能。

mainPoints.point: 未来的路线图侧重于 MLX 集成和高级自主智能 (Agentic AI) 用例，以实现持续创新。
mainPoints.explanation: Hugging Face 计划深化与 Apple 的 MLX 框架的集成，并探索自主智能 (Agentic AI) 工作流程，旨在进一步增强 Apple 平台上的本地 AI 功能和开发者效率。

keyQuotes: 两年前，我们发布了 [`swift-transformers`](https://github.com/huggingface/swift-transformers)（！）的⽬标是⽀持 Apple 开发者，并帮助他们将本地 LLM 集成到他们的应用中。
`swift-transformers` 是一个 Swift 库，旨在减少开发者在 Apple 芯片平台（包括 iPhones）上使用本地模型时遇到的阻力。
1.  0 版本标志着软件包的稳定性。开发者正在 `swift-transformers` 的基础上构建应用程序，第一个主要版本认可了这些用例，并使版本号与现实情况相符。
说到 Jinja，我们非常自豪地宣布，我们与 John Mai 合作创建了他的优秀 Swift Jinja 库的下一个版本。
老实说，我们不知道。但我们确实对探索 MLX 非常感兴趣，因为这通常是开发者开始在原生应用中使用机器学习的常用方法。
